newsSince: The memory super-cycle deepened in TSMC's favor for niche memory: Macronix reports strong NOR demand for AI servers/HPC, has shifted to monthly NAND pricing amid a 'massive shortage,' guides full capacity utilization in 2H26, and ended a ten-quarter losing streak in Q1 (GM 40.8%, NAND rev +382% YoY on Samsung's MLC exit). On June 8 it announced ASIL-D/ISO-21434 certification of its ArmorFlash MX78 secure NOR for AI/edge/auto.

sources.label: TrendForce - Macronix NAND +382% YoY, monthly pricing
BigGo - Macronix ends 10-quarter losing streak, full load 2H26
PR Newswire - ArmorFlash MX78 ASIL-D certification (Jun 8)

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node bottleneck lens: the NAND/NOR shortage that broadens the memory chokepoint into niche flash is genuinely strengthening - monthly pricing, full 2H26 load, NAND +382% YoY, plus the June 8 secure-NOR ASIL-D win for AI/edge. This is fresh fundamental evidence, not just price, supporting a modest up from a low rank-98 slot.
verdicts.bull: NOR/SLC-NAND shortage with monthly price hikes and Samsung MLC exit; capacity full into 2H26; profitability inflection after 10 down quarters.
verdicts.neutral: Small ($8.8B) niche-memory player; capacity capped ~25k wpm so upside is price- not volume-led.
verdicts.bear: Notoriously cyclical memory at +297% YTD; valuation discovery already steep and a price reversal would bite.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Monthly NOR/NAND price hikes and full 2H26 utilization
verdicts.primaryRisk: Memory-cycle rollover; capacity cap limits volume upside
